mission: Own Aquarium Synth patch authoring, analysis, preset mapping, and Faust generation in C# for the Aquarium/Vortice engine path.

own_here: DX7 measured carrier output amplitude curve
DX7 ROM COM output compensation cut
DX7 block-interpolated envelope applied-gain trace helper

taste: Patch scripts should be terse but lower into explicit, inspectable graph data.
Compiler abstractions should remove repeated musical structure, not hide DSP behavior.
Generated Faust should be boring enough to diff and debug.
Realtime-facing work must assume allocation, locks, file I/O, console I/O, and OS calls are hostile.

reference_strategy.purpose: Use external synths and DSP corpora as parity pressure for Aquarium's patch DSL and Faust emitter: the DSL must reproduce behavior in terse, readable Aquarium syntax, not clone foreign internals.
priority_order: DX7/Dexed SysEx for FM operator topology
ZynAddSubFX instruments for additive, subtractive, PAD, formant, and layered patches
Faust examples/libraries for general DSP graph topology
Surge XT for modern hybrid synth modulation
Cardinal/VCV-style patches for modular cable/rate semantics
reference_strategy.rule: Add DSL abstractions only when reference rebuilds prove the missing invariant. Do not consume references that cannot be backed by parity tests; parsing alone is inventory, not evidence of expressivity.

dx7_reference_path.status: DX7 SysEx parser extracts voice edit buffers, packed voices, packed 32-voice banks, six operators, envelopes, algorithm, feedback, oscillator sync, LFO, transpose, voice name, checksum validation, ReferencePatch features, algorithm topology from the reverse-engineered OPS algorithm ROM table, envelope-to-ADSR approximations, measured carrier output amplitude, isolated two-op modulation route scale, summed/cascaded route scale, calibrated feedback amount table, ratio detune lowering, voice transpose lowering, fixed-frequency operator lowering, ROM COM output compensation cut, topology-aware route lowering, staged operator envelopes with per-segment curves, raw and block-interpolated DX7 envelope tracing, applied-envelope curved lowering with optional sustain floor, two Aquarium DSL rebuild attempts, first-class OperatorGraph support, public-domain SysEx fixture coverage, dexed-py reference rendering, project-authored Dexed patch rendering, Aquarium candidate rendering through Faust-generated C#, one thresholded rendered parity test for analog1.syx voice 13, a hard PRC SYNTH1 parity probe with listening WAV artifacts and log-mel <= 0.18, project-authored algorithm-8 summed-stack/cascade/summed-pair parity, rendered community parity for Mooger, Piano Bass, RES SYNTH1, and ANLGSYN 1 with a sustained high-band gate, and renderable delayed operator feedback.
dx7_reference_path.lesson: Algorithm 32's additive six-carrier shape maps cleanly to six Aquarium voices. Algorithm 8 justified OperatorGraph: operators, modulation edges, carriers, and feedback belong to a graph authority, not Voice fields. The first rendered parity rung is behavioral and sine-like, not exact DX7 operator execution. A harder algorithm-8 target exposed that feedback must be delayed recursion in Faust, not a direct cyclic self-reference. Static DX7 output-level approximation helped import/rebuild math but was mathematically wrong for carrier amplitude. A single-carrier Dexed sweep shows carrier amplitude follows 2^((outputLevel - 99) / 8). Isolated two-op DX7 FM maps full-level modulation to Aquarium route index 6.275; summed/cascaded algorithm-8 branches now use 6.0 after PRC listening, a project-authored combined stack probe, and split cascade/summed-pair probes showed lower values were under-bright. Feedback lowers through a fitted table instead of PRC-local constants; ANLGSYN 1 showed max feedback value 7 needs a hotter Aquarium amount 2.2 to recover buzzy high bands, while value 5 remains 0.19 for PRC. ANLGSYN 1 then exposed a sustained-buzz gap: output soft clip helped but did not stop mid-note drift; max-feedback source operators need an applied-envelope sustain floor so the feedback loop stays driven after the attack. Ratio-mode detune belongs in operator frequency lowering. DX7 voice transpose belongs in graph base frequency: transpose 24 is neutral, transpose 12 lowers MIDI note 60 to 130.8128 Hz. DX7 fixed-frequency operators are absolute Hz, not note ratios; ANLGSYN 1 proved fixed op1/op3 must lower to ratios around 0.007615/0.009227 against a 130.8128 Hz graph base to preserve low buzzing modulation. ROM COM carrier compensation was cut: user listening found no-COM renders harmonically closer and mainly too quiet, so `OperatorOutputCompensation` returns unity and graph gain owns loudness recovery. Project-authored Dexed patch rendering now gives lawful calibration targets. Reusing the operator output curve for EG levels was tested and cut because it failed hard-target log-mel. Direct envelope tracing shows current linear `env=rl` cannot express DX7 EG spike/drop behavior. Dexed-style audio samples EG once per 64-sample block and interpolates applied gain, so raw EG parity alone is not audio parity. Staged operator envelopes now support linear/exponential segments and supranormal levels. `ApproximateAppliedRateLevelEnvelope` uses that surface and now picks the first near-peak before gate so slower rising modulators hit early instead of swelling late; it can also preserve a floor for max-feedback source sustain. Community gains with no COM: Mooger 0.75, Piano Bass 0.90, RES SYNTH1 0.75. PRC log-mel is down to 0.13874851 with RMS near unity; DX1 LEAD B and MELLOWSOLO sound closer by ear under no-COM/gain-restored lowering but still fail numeric parity gates.
dx7_reference_path.next: Use the now-validated algorithm-8 route scale, transpose lowering, and fixed-frequency lowering as fixed inputs while isolating remaining community-voice timbre mismatch, then expand to broader public-domain banks.

note_and_envelope_model.rule: Notes own frequency, gate duration, and host/MIDI source. Envelopes own ADSR level shape.
note_and_envelope_model.sfxr_mapping: SFXR sustain is note gate duration. SFXR punch maps to lower ADSR sustain level plus voice gain compensation so the implicit attack peak stays at 1 and the held level sits below it.
note_and_envelope_model.faust_surface: One-shot patches use generated ADSR over patch age and note gate duration. MIDI mono/poly patches emit Faust's standard freq/gain/gate controls and [midi:on][nvoices:n] metadata so Faust architectures own note allocation.

runtime_parameters.rule: Patch parameters are runtime controls, not compile-time constants.
runtime_parameters.faust_surface: Faust UI controls expose parameter zones that hosts can update with setParamValue/getParamValue-style APIs.
runtime_parameters.aquarium_requirement: Aquarium DSL patches declare stable exposed parameters with path/default/min/max/step metadata so callers can vary sound without recompiling.
runtime_parameters.binding_rule: Parameter binding uses field-site references such as `lpf=@/macro/brightness` and `/opgraphs/0/operators/2/level`; the field owns meaning and the parameter owns the runtime value. Avoid target-routing semantics unless a new graph authority is deliberately mapped.

last_verified.result: 74 passed; ROM COM carrier compensation cut, graph gain owns community loudness, RES SYNTH1 added to passing community set
